title: pleomele aurantiaca
intro: Pleomele aurantiaca, also known as the orange passion flower or orange ti plant, is a type of flowering plant native to the Pacific Islands, belonging to the Asparagaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pleomele aurantiaca include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing antioxidant properties that help protect against cell damage. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are typically made by extracting the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or oil.
